ul.li: Host: Input/output buffers + 2 pinned staging buffers
Device: 2 input buffers + intermediate + output + window + work area
No ring buffers: Direct pipeline flow
Host: Input/output buffers + pinned ring buffers (98 KB) — zero-copy DMA via peek_frame() (Phase 1.1)
Ring buffers: Per-channel pinned memory, 3× NFFT capacity
Use case: Real-time streaming with arbitrary chunk sizes

div.p: Both modes use the same device memory layout and processing pipeline. The streaming overhead is entirely in host memory for ring buffer management.
BatchExecutor Mode: High-throughput, minimal overhead Total Memory: ~164 KB (nfft=2048, channels=2)

ul.li: frames = temporal dimension (FFT windows over time)

div.p: Complex format Hermitian symmetry Only N/2+1 bins stored Saves 50% memory
Page-locked memory Enables faster PCIe transfers (~25 GB/s) cudaHostAlloc()
